commands.command: as noted in the review, the del (0x7f) handling is already covered by pi-tui's `matcheskey(data, key.backspace)`. the actual issue #92777 is about backspace failing after agent response rendering, which this pr does not address. the maintainer options suggested pausing or closing this attempt. i'm closing this pr to focus on the actual post-response tui failure path. the linked issue #92777 should be investigated for the root cause in the input/editor lifecycle path after agent responses.
commands.freeform_prompt: As noted in the review, the DEL (0x7f) handling is already covered by pi-tui's `matchesKey(data, Key.backspace)`. The actual issue #92777 is about Backspace failing after agent response rendering, which this PR does not address. The maintainer options suggested pausing or closing this attempt. I'm closing this PR to focus on the actual post-response TUI failure path. The linked issue #92777 should be investigated for the root cause in the input/editor lifecycle path after agent responses.

target.title: fix(tui): recognize DEL (0x7f) as backspace alongside BS (0x08)
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Linux/WSL2 terminals send the DEL character (0x7f) for the backspace key
instead of BS (0x08). The backspace deduper only recognised BS, causing
DEL-based backspace input to be lost after the dedupe window closed.

Closes #92777

## Changes

- **`src/tui/tui.ts`** — added `data !== "\x7f"` to the backspace deduper
  guard so DEL characters are treated the same as BS characters.

## Evidence

- **Tests**: Vitest 63/63 passed (~37s).
  `src/tui/tui.test.ts` — existing tests already cover `\x7f` (DEL) as a
  recognised backspace; this fix ensures they are properly deduped.
